channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Tents arrive for survivors of earthquake in high-altitude, wintry Tibet that killed 126
item.description: Rescue workers in Tibet are searching for more survivors and victims, one day after a strong earthquake leveled thousands of houses and killed at least 126 people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader says his son-in-law has been kidnapped in Caracas
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Self-exiled Venezuelan opposition leader Edmundo González, who claims to have defeated President Nicolás Maduro in last year’s presidential election, said his son-in-law was kidnapped Tuesday in Venezuela's capital.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's Conservative leader slams Trump's '51st state' idea
item.description: Pierre Poilievre, leader of Canada’s Conservative Party, shot back at President-elect Trump’s idea of making his country America’s 51st state. “Canada will never be the 51st state. Period. We are a great and independent country,” Poilievre wrote Tuesday in a statement on social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: At least 9 miners are trapped in a coal mine in India's northeastern Assam state
item.description: Indian authorities have summoned the army to help rescue at least nine workers who are trapped inside a flooded coal mine in the country's northeastern Assam state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump reinforces 'all hell will break out' if hostages not returned by inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump once again threatened 'all hell will break out' if the hostages in Gaza are not freed by the time he enters office, though he refused to detail what actions he will take.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden administration eases sanctions on transitional Syrian government after Assad ouster
item.description: The Biden administration has eased sanctions blocking transactions with the transitional Syrian government following the ousting of longtime dictator Bashar Assad. The move marks a gesture of outreach between the U.S. and Hay’at Tahrir al Sham (HTS), designated as a terrorist group in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US determines Sudan paramilitary committed genocide, sanctions leader
item.description: The Biden administration on Tuesday sanctioned the top general of Sudan’s Rapid Support Forces (RSF) and determined the paramilitary group has committed genocide in the country, as well as crimes against humanity and ethnic cleansing. While the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Denmark stresses Greenland not for sale after Trump Jr. arrives on island
item.description: Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen stressed that Greenland is not for sale after President-elect Trump’s oldest son, Donald Trump Jr., arrived on the Arctic island.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Trump threatens economic, not military force, to annex Canada
item.description: President-elect Trump said Tuesday he was not considering using military force to make Canada part of the United States after repeatedly musing about the idea of the country becoming the 51st state. Instead, Trump said he intended to use "economic force" against the neighbor to the north.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Ireland joins South Africa's ICJ genocide case against Israel
item.description: Ireland filed a declaration of intervention Monday in support of South Africa’s genocide case against Israel in the International Court of Justice (ICJ).
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump: 'All hell will break out' if Hamas hostages not released by inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump repeated his warnings that all hell will break loose in the Middle East if hostages kidnapped from Israel and held by Hamas in the Gaza Strip are not released before his inauguration on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: France marks 10 years since deadly Charlie Hebdo attack
item.description: PARIS (AP) — A decade after Islamic extremist gunmen stormed the offices of the satirical newspaper Charlie Hebdo in a deadly assault that shook France to its core and ignited a global outcry in defense of freedom of speech, the nation paused on Tuesday to honor the victims and renew its resolve to
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senegal and Chad say ousting of French troops was their sovereign decision
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Senegalese and Chadian leaders said that the ousting of French troops from their territories was their sovereign decision, rejecting remarks from French President Emmanuel Macron that suggested otherwise.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK and BABACAR DIONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tibet earthquake kills at least 126 people and leaves many trapped
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A strong earthquake shook a high-altitude region of western China and areas of Nepal on Tuesday, damaging hundreds of houses, littering streets with rubble and killing at least 126 people in Tibet. Many others were trapped as dozens of aftershocks shook the remote region.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: O'Leary: Trump US-Canada merger an interesting concept
item.description: "Shark Tank” star Kevin O’Leary said Monday that the concept of the U.S. and Canada merging into one country is "very interesting." “I went to Palm Beach for a couple of days. On Saturday, we discussed Canada,” O’Leary, who is Canadian, said Monday evening on "The Hill" on NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Why more frequent cold blasts could be coming from global warming
item.description: Frigid air that normally stays trapped in the Arctic has escaped, plunging deep into the United States for an extended visit that is expected to provoke teeth-chattering but not be record-shattering.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First international commercial flight since Assad's ouster lands at Syria's main airport
item.description: The first international commercial flight since the fall of former Syrian President Bashar Assad has landed at Damascus airport.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hurdles remain as Israel and Hamas once again inch toward a ceasefire deal
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel and Hamas once again appear to be inching toward a ceasefire that could wind down the 15-month war in Gaza and bring home dozens of Israelis held hostage there. Both Israel and Hamas are under pressure from outgoing U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who is Pierre Poilievre? Canada's Conservative leader seeking to become next prime minister after Trudeau exit
item.description: The leader of Canada's Conservative party, Pierre Poilievre, has been leading in the polls and is favorite to become the country's next prime minister following the exit of Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: A far-right leader could soon take the helm in EU member country Austria. Here's why it matters
item.description: VIENNA, Austria (AP) — A party that advocates an end to economic sanctions against Russia and has called for the “re-migration of uninvited foreigners” could soon give Austria its first government led by the far right since World War II, with a leader who has a provocative style at its helm.
item.dc:creator: PHILIPP JENNE and JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 3 killed in West Bank 'shooting spree' including Israeli police officer: reports
item.description: A shooting spree by Palestinian terrorists in the West Bank has left three Israelis dead as a manhunt is ongoing for the suspects.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nigel Farage responds after Elon Musk declares he 'doesn't have what it takes' to lead Reform UK Party
item.description: Elon Musk targeted Reform UK party leader Nigel Farage, saying he "doesn't have what it takes" and the party "needs a new leader.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea carries out first missile launch test since Trump's election
item.description: North Korea launched its first missile test since Election Day on Monday, strongly implying that it does not plan to play nice with President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about the siege outside South Korea's presidential compound
item.description: A standoff between rival government forces outside the presidential compound in South Korea is a startling development, even for observers used to the country’s famously rough and tumble politics.
item.dc:creator: Foster Klug, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau to announce resignation: Reports
item.description: It remains unclear whether Justin Trudeau will leave immediately or stay on as prime minister until a new leader is selected, the report added.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: China fires shot across Trump's bow with defense sanctions
item.description: It could be a sign of a brewing tit-for-tat as the two superpowers jockey for economic and military leverage.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, WGN 9

item.title: The world welcomes 2025 with light shows, embraces and ice plunges
item.description: The New Year’s Eve ball dropped in soggy Times Square, where thousands of revelers stuck it out in heavy rain to celebrate.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, CEDAR ATTANASIO and CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, NewsNation
